title: Untold Encounters of the Random Kind - An Invaluable Tool for the Solo Gamer
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=o0dULDtKQ-8

Daniel (@dungeondive) makes an enthusiastic case for Loke Battle Mats' Untold Encounters of the Random Kind — a 1,000-plus-encounter book he calls an invaluable, criminally underrated solo tool that deserves to sit on every table beside Table Fables. His chief gripe isn't with the book but its obscurity: barely reviewed, and absent from the solo channels where it belongs.

It's fully system-agnostic — encounters described in plain keywords (damage as minor/light/major/lethal, difficulty tiers, challenge ratings) with a 5e conversion layer bolted on — so it drops into White Box, Scarlet Heroes or nearly any fantasy game. The content splits into three great sections: Town (ten districts from castle ward to under-city sewers), Wilderness (by region — arctic, desert, forest, sea), and Dungeon (by encounter type — boons, doors, enemies, traps, intrigues). Each section pairs d100 tables with an alphabetical A-Z of detailed write-ups, then closes with a staged adventure-builder: roll plot, theme, antagonists, complications and a number of escalating stages to assemble a whole session.

He demos a few rolls — a noble eyeing graveyard land whose boundary wall hides protective wards, a wilderness apparel-stash mystery — to show how each entry seeds an adventure. For solo play, he rates it essential.

----

Which random-encounter book or generator has earned permanent table space for you — and is anything as underrated as Daniel claims this one is?
